name: Light
subject: physics
summary: Light travels straight, reflects, refracts, and splits into colours. Laws of reflection, mirrors, lenses, and the human eye.

crushed.title: Light travels in straight lines
crushed.text: Ray model — light goes straight until it hits something. Pinhole camera proves this.

crushed.title: Reflection: angle in = angle out
crushed.text: Angle of incidence = angle of reflection. Same for plane, concave, and convex mirrors.

crushed.title: Refraction = bending at boundary
crushed.text: Light slows in denser medium → bends. Pencil in water looks broken.

crushed.title: Dispersion
crushed.text: White light splits into VIBGYOR through a prism. Violet bends most, red least.

crushed.title: Eye = natural camera
crushed.text: Cornea + lens focus image on retina. Myopia (near) → concave lens. Hypermetropia (far) → convex lens.

items.label: Law 1
items.detail: Incident ray, reflected ray, and normal all lie in the same plane

items.label: Law 2
items.detail: Angle of incidence (∠i) = Angle of reflection (∠r)

items.label: Plane mirror
items.detail: Virtual, erect, same size image. Laterally inverted

items.label: Concave mirror
items.detail: Converges light. Used in torches, dentist mirrors, solar furnaces

items.label: Convex mirror
items.detail: Diverges light. Wider field of view — rear-view mirrors, shop security

items.label: Convex (converging)
items.detail: Thicker in middle. Focuses parallel rays to a point

items.label: Concave (diverging)
items.detail: Thinner in middle. Spreads parallel rays apart

flows.title: Tracing a Reflected Ray
flows.description: Follow a light ray hitting a plane mirror step by step.

steps.label: Incident ray
steps.description: Ray travels in straight line toward mirror surface.

steps.label: Normal
steps.description: Normal is line perpendicular to mirror at point of incidence.

steps.label: Measure ∠i
steps.description: Angle between incident ray and normal.

steps.label: Reflect
steps.description: Reflected ray makes equal angle on other side of normal.

steps.label: Image
steps.description: Extend reflected ray back — virtual image appears behind mirror.

flows.title: White Light Through a Prism
flows.description: Newton's experiment — white light is made of all colours.

steps.label: White light
steps.description: Single white beam enters triangular glass prism.

steps.label: Refraction
steps.description: Light slows in glass — bends toward normal at first surface.

steps.label: Split
steps.description: Violet (short λ) bends most. Red (long λ) bends least.

steps.label: Spectrum
steps.description: Seven colours spread out — visible spectrum.

steps.label: Conclusion
steps.description: White light is a mixture of all visible colours.

flows.title: How the Human Eye Focuses
flows.description: Light enters the eye and forms an image on the retina.

steps.label: Light enters
steps.description: Cornea does most of the initial bending of light.

steps.label: Pupil
steps.description: Iris adjusts pupil size — more light in dark, less in bright.

steps.label: Lens
steps.description: Crystalline lens adjusts shape (accommodation) to focus on retina.

steps.label: Retina
steps.description: Real, inverted image on retina — rod and cone cells detect it.

steps.label: Brain
steps.description: Optic nerve sends signals — brain interprets as upright image.

keyTerms.definition: Bouncing back of light when it hits a surface
keyTerms.analogy: A ball bouncing off a wall

keyTerms.definition: Bending of light when it passes from one medium to another
keyTerms.analogy: A car slowing and turning when entering mud from road

keyTerms.definition: Splitting of white light into its component colours
keyTerms.analogy: A prism revealing the hidden rainbow inside white light
Eye focuses images too soon — concave lens pushes focus back

demos.title: Prism — White Light to Rainbow
demos.description: A glass prism bends each colour differently, splitting white light into VIBGYOR.

steps.label: Sunlight in lab
steps.description: Wide view: white sunlight enters through the window toward a prism.

steps.label: Light hits prism
steps.description: Zoom in: the ray refracts as it enters and leaves the glass.

steps.label: VIBGYOR spectrum
steps.description: Each colour bends differently — a rainbow spreads on the screen.

chapterSummary.overview: Light enables us to see objects. This chapter covers laws of reflection, plane and curved mirrors, kaleidoscopes, sunlight dispersion through a prism (VIBGYOR spectrum), the human eye, vision defects, and care of eyes.
keyPoints: Light travels in straight lines; reflection follows angle of incidence = angle of reflection.
Plane mirror: virtual, erect, same-size image; lateral inversion.
White light splits into seven colours through a prism — violet bends most, red least.
Eye: cornea, iris, lens, retina; image formed on retina, signal sent to brain via optic nerve.
Vision defects: myopia (near-sighted), hypermetropia (far-sighted) corrected with lenses.
chapterSummary.examFocus: Draw ray diagrams for plane mirror. Explain dispersion of light. Label parts of the human eye.
